title: AFRICAN REFUGEE IMMIGRANT ORGANIZATION - 211info
text: AFRICAN REFUGEE IMMIGRANT ORGANIZATION AFRICAN REFUGEE IMMIGRANT ORGANIZATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides community events such as youth soccer, open gym, tutoring, community-specific workshops (topics include traditions, art, music, history, or language), leadership workshops, and clothing donation drives. Also assists in enrollment in the Oregon Health Plan (OHP) and helps individuals who are job searching, resume building, and with job application form completion. Provides information about rent assistance programs. 7535 NE Glisan Street Portland, OR 972133905 Eligibility: Varies by service with a focus on African refugees and immigrant families Hours: Phone: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Events: Varies by service Email (971) 430-6193 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (971) 430-6193 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call or email for more information. Visit website for the current event schedule. FEES: None DOCUMENTS: Varies by service LANGUAGES: English, Maay Maay, Somali, Swahili, Lingala, and French AREA SERVED: Clackamas, Multnomah, and Washington Counties Services: Cultural Heritage Groups * Refugees/Entrants/Asylees Cultural Heritage Programs * African Community Cultural Heritage Programs * Immigrants Leadership Development * African Community Leadership Development * Immigrants Leadership Development * Refugees/Entrants/Asylees Navigator Programs * African Community Navigator Programs * Immigrants Navigator Programs * Refugees/Entrants/Asylees Parenting Skills Classes * African Community Parenting Skills Classes * Immigrants Parenting Skills Classes * Refugees/Entrants/Asylees Prejob Guidance * African Community Prejob Guidance * Immigrants Prejob Guidance * Refugees/Entrants/Asylees Specialized Information and Referral * African Community Specialized Information and Referral * Immigrants Specialized Information and Referral * Refugees/Entrants/Asylees Youth Enrichment Programs * African Community Youth Enrichment Programs * Immigrants Youth Enrichment Programs * Refugees/Entrants/Asylees Other Services Offered At This Location: FOOD - FREE FOOD MARKET If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service.
